title: India launches ‘Op Amistad’ to aid quake-hit Venezuela; sends relief material, medical assistance
content: The 41-member team, comprising experienced rescue personnel and medical professionals, will work closely with the Venezuelan authorities in search and rescue operations

title: Cleaning Godavari a challenge ahead of 2027 Pushkarams
content: The waters of the sacred river of Akhanda Godavari have been declared unsuitable either for drinking or a holy dip due to contamination with effluents being released from industries and untreated sewage water, threatening the survival of the river ecosystem and forcing four lakh residents of the city to drink, while traditional fisherfolk abandoned the river for drinking, bathing on the Eastern bank.

title: Congress appoints Rajendra Pal Gautam, Sanjay Dutt as AICC in-charge for UP, Haryana
content: Mr. Gautam replaces Avinash Pande in Uttar Pradesh, while Mr. Dutt replaces senior party leader Hariprasad in Haryana

title: Exhibition to pay tribute to legacy of Narasimha Rao
content: The event will include an exhibition showcasing a collection of rare photographs, official documents, speeches, correspondence and archival material tracing the journey of the late former PM.

title: One dead in Agartala blast; State govt. orders probe
content: The fact-finding committee, led by the Secretary, Urban Develop Department, has been directed to submit a report within seven days

title: Kuki-Zo Council’s apology for killings is not enough: Manipur Naga youth body
content: The All Naga Students’ Association, Manipur, slams the Kuki-Zo Council for using a derogatory term while referring to the slain Liangmai Naga civilians

title: Beijing’s ties with Bangladesh does not target any third party, says Chinese Foreign Ministry
content: China expressed support to Bangladesh to take up the comprehensive Teesta river project; Rahman conveys Dhaka’s commitment to ‘one China’ policy; earlier China backed Bangladesh in supporting its ‘sovereignty and territorial integrity’

title: Nearly 6 crore names have been deleted in first yearof SIR of electoral rolls
content: Post Bihar SIR, its voters' list was pruned by nearly 65 lakh amid allegations by the Opposition and activists that the poll authority was working at the behest of the BJP to disenfranchise citizens for want of documents

title: West Bengal Congress to step up protests over NTA question paper leaks
content: Congress leadership in West Bengal will hold protests on August 1 demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan as the part of nation wide programme ‘Chhatron Ki Goonj’ said Congress leader Prasenjit Bose

title: Pharma firm licence revoked over Kota post-partum deaths; WHO seeks report
content: Five women died between May 5 and May 17 at the JK Lone Hospital and the Super Speciality Wing of Government Medical College in Kota, Rajasthan, following Caesarean section deliveries

title: Supreme Court lays to rest 30-year-old case of man’s death over a ₹500 watch
content: The top court Bench headed by Justice Ujjal Bhuyan closed criminal proceedings in the culpable homicide case after noting that two of the three accused persons have died during the pendency of the appeal

title: NFSA amendments: 7 kg of foodgrains for a person, and maximum 35 kg for a household
content: The Centre has proposed revising the foodgrain entitlement for AAY beneficiaries under the NFSA. While it says the change will ensure fairer distribution, critics fear it could reduce allocations for many households

title: Himachal should be given 7.19 % share in Chandigarh: Sukhu
content: Chief Minister raises Chandigarh share, BBMB dues and Shanan project during meeting with Punjab Governor and UT Administrator

title: UCC to be implemented in Bengal through proper procedure, says Suvendu
content: Implementation of UCC was promised by Union Home Minister Amit Shah during the campaign for West Bengal Assembly election.

title: Myanmar replaces Afghanistan as key opium source, impact seen on India’s eastern border: NCB
content: Myanmar’s Golden Triangle has expanded as an opiate supplier and meth hub, with the drugs then flowing across a porous border into Manipur, Mizoram, and Nagaland; drone-based drug trafficking from Pakistan is also a rising concern

title: The rules that failed 15 lives in Lucknow
content: On June 22, a fire broke out in a building in Uttar Pradesh’s capital city, claiming 15 lives, most of them students and staff of an animation centre. According to eyewitnesses, bystanders, and survivors, the building lacked basic fire safety systems. Mayank Kumar reports on the tragedy and the regulatory gaps it exposes amid rapid urbanisation in India’s most populous State

title: Extension of Kochi Water Metro ferries to Kadamakudy by Onam
content: Relocation of Chinese fishing nets along the route and dredging of the terminal premises are the remaining works before services can begin, says KWML chief

title: Names of six personnel who died in action during Op Sindoor inscribed at National War Memorial
content: This is the first time since the military operation conducted by the Indian armed forces in May 2025 that the government has released the names of these six personnel

title: T.N. govt. permits increased intake in arts and science colleges
content: However, the institutions were barred from seeking sanction for additional posts in view of the increased intake of students

title: Commuters protest at bus depot in Arani for extension of bus services
content: Commuters said that they have been demanding extension of the bus services and a direct bus service to Vellore town for more than three decades

title: Agencies detect rise in gold smuggling after import duty hike
content: A Customs officer said teams rely heavily on profiling, behavioural analysis and intelligence inputs to identify suspects

title: Identity cards for Class 10, 12 students to be issued in first phase: Sengottaiyan
content: As students were required to produce community certificates every year for various purposes, the government has decided to introduce identity cards, which would integrate all student details, the Minister said.

title: Activists flag safety, accommodation lapses after Tiruvallur seafood unit ammonia leak
content: The workers, many of them young women from Odisha and Assam aged between 19 and 25, were off duty and resting in the dormitory when the leak occurred

title: A convert to Islam cannot claim the status of Backward Class Muslim, rules Madras High Court
content: Categorising certain sects as Backward and the remaining as Forward is antithetical to Quranic injunctions. Islam seeks to establish an egalitarian society, says Madurai Bench

title: Centre asks hospitals to disclose post-organ transplant survival data
content: Registered transplant hospitals across the country were told to display the crucial data on the home page of their respective websites

title: Home Minister Amit Shah says next three years decisive in fight against drugs, releases vision document
content: The NDPS Act and Rules will be updated to address emerging challenges and address regulatory gaps while promoting a more reformative approach towards persons suffering from addiction, vision document says

title: Opposition to step up protest against ruling BJP at Thiruavananthapuram Corporation
content: LDF files a complaint to Principal Secretary, alleging swearing-in on Wednesday was not conducted as per norms; UDF's parliamentary party meeting decides to move no-confidence motion against BJP-led administration

title: MDMK’s general body to take a decision on alliance  on Saturday
content: There are expectations that the MDMK may ask its MLAs — T.M. Rajendran and  Senthil Selvan to resign from the membership of the Assembly as they contested on the DMK’s ‘Rising Sun’ symbol in the 2026 election

title: Students in Classes 7, 8, & 9 can continue learning foreign languages this year: Education Ministry sources
content: These students do not have switch to an extra native language till they complete Class 10, the sources added; the clarification comes over a month after CBSE stated that students in Class 9 must study three languages, of which two should be native to India, in the 2026-27 academic year

title: Rahul marks two years as Leader of the Opposition; vows to continue fighting for the people
content: The Opposition leader targets the Prime Minister over his praise of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, saying the students who died due to NEET paper leak are victims of a broken system

title: Drug menace threatens society, future generations must be protected: Kerala CM
content: Kerala government is adopting a systematic approach to curb drug trafficking and dismantle the networks behind it, says Satheesan

title: Ernakulam crosses 1-lakh mark in Census self-enumeration
content: ‘Despite crossing the one-lakh mark, the self-enumeration response in Ernakulam remains well below the district administration’s expectations with just four days left before the June 30 deadline’

title: Permanent TET exemption sought for in-service teachers in A.P.
content: Forum of Registered Teacher Organisations leaders submit representations to MPs, urging them to amend the RTE Act, 2009 to provide the permanent exemption

title: BJP accuses Congress-led govt. in Kerala of promoting alcohol consumption
content: BJP MP and spokesperson Sudhanshu Trivedi described the decision to reduce excise tax on low-alcohol liquor by nearly half as “disastrous”

title: Muharram processions held across Kashmir; CM Omar, L-G Sinha join mourners
content: Shia Muslims hold processions to mark Ashura in Srinagar and other parts of J&K; marches carried out under tight security

title: All eight accused in Ram Temple donation embezzlement case sent to judicial custody
content: Despite rumours that Champat Rai has resigned as general secretary of the Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, no official confirmation yet; arrested accused include close aides of Mr. Champat Rai

title: Death toll in Kolkata warehouse collapse rises to 16
content: Rescue operations are being carried out for the third consecutive day at the site of the accident, by NDRF, Indian Army, Kolkata Police and other agencies

title: Civil society group proposes water security commission amid concerns over shrinking waterbodies in Kashmir
content: Centre’s clearances for felling nearly 2.8 million trees, coupled with large-scale infrastructure in hill regions, could destabilise fragile mountain ecosystems, says the chairman of the civil society group

title: U.P. Rajeev appointed Director of Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre
content: Dr. Rajeev has extensive experience in the area of rocket control and guidance, and has led the mission designs and mission simulations of major launch vehicles of the ISRO

title: 'Gravest sin': Priyanka Gandhi says Ram Temple donation fraud has 'stunned entire nation'
content: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra has called for a thorough investigation into the alleged irregularities surrounding donations to the Ram Temple. She highlighted the need for severe consequences for those responsible and raised concerns about whether lower-tier staff could act independently without higher-ups' influence. Gandhi asserted that the inquiry should be transparent and hold all involved parties accountable for their actions.

title: Watch: Venezuela's twin earthquakes leave nearly 1,000 dead, trail of despair
content: Venezuela reels from twin earthquakes, with the death toll tragically climbing to 920 and over 50,000 reported missing amidst widespread devastation. Rescue efforts are in full swing, with international aid pouring in from nations like the US and India, which launched Operation Amistad. The humanitarian crisis deepens, particularly for the millions of children affected, as the search for survivors continues under mountains of rubble.

title: 'We failed': Abhishek Sharma explains India's shock defeat to Ireland
content: Ireland secured a historic 34-run victory over reigning T20 World Cup champions India in the first T20I at Belfast. Despite a blistering 50 off 20 balls from Abhishek Sharma, India faltered, failing to adapt to the conditions. Ireland posted 182/9, with Lorcan Tucker leading the charge. India were bowled out for 148, giving Ireland a 1-0 series lead.

title: ‘Accused’s ego was hurt’: Passenger recalls 21-year-old getting stabbed to death in Mumbai local
content: A horrific murder unfolded on a Mumbai local train after a heated argument over a compartment door escalated into a fatal stabbing. Eyewitnesses recount the brutal attack on 21-year-old Mayank Lohar by Roshan Suvarna, who allegedly brandished a knife at other passengers before fleeing. The incident has left commuters traumatized, with calls for the strictest punishment for the accused.

title: 'Profound change required': Volkswagen plans 100,000 job cuts and four plant closures
content: Volkswagen is planning a massive job cut of 100,000 positions and the closure of four German plants. This drastic overhaul, the most significant in its history, aims to counter intense competition from Chinese electric vehicle makers. The automaker also intends to reduce research and development spending by 15% as it faces declining market share in China and growing pressure from rivals expanding globally.

title: 'Silver missing, Rs 80 lakh recovered': 8 in judicial custody over Ram Mandir donation fraud
content: Eight individuals arrested in connection with alleged donation theft at the Ayodhya Ram temple have been remanded to judicial custody until Monday. Authorities recovered over Rs 79 lakh in cash from most of the accused. While no government employees are currently implicated, the investigation's scope may broaden, with charges including criminal conspiracy and breach of trust.

title: NY Mayor Zohran Mamdani files motion to ban delivery app Motoclick: What lawsuit says
content: New York City is seeking to ban food delivery platform Motoclick for allegedly paying workers as little as $3.67 per hour, a fraction of the mandated $22.13 minimum wage. City officials filed a motion for a preliminary injunction, aiming to halt the company's operations unless it complies with labor laws.

title: Siya wanted Chetan but family fixed Ketan: Cops reveal probe details in Pune murder
content: A Pune realtor's murder investigation reveals a complex motive involving a secret relationship clashing with a family-arranged marriage. Police allege Siya Goyal, engaged to Ketan Agarwal, was close to co-accused Chetan Chaudhary. Investigators believe a forced marriage and a hidden affair led to the crime, with deleted chats and a staged push at Lohagad Fort central to the plot. Siya's brother has been questioned extensively.

title: Netanyahu says Israel to remain in south Lebanon despite US-backed peace framework
content: Israel will keep troops in southern Lebanon until Hezbollah is disarmed, despite a new trilateral agreement with Lebanon and the US aimed at ending conflict. Prime Minister Netanyahu confirmed Israel's commitment to the security zone, while Lebanon's army will gradually take control as non-state armed groups disarm. The US hailed the accord as a crucial first step towards broader peace, though Hezbollah has rejected the negotiations.

title: Swiss glaciers shrink as climate crisis fuels extreme heat across Europe
content: Switzerland's glaciers are facing unprecedented melting, with "glacier loss day" arriving exceptionally early this year due to a prolonged European heatwave, warm May, and insufficient winter snowfall. Experts warn that this rapid ice loss, exacerbated by persistent high temperatures, mirrors the dire conditions of 2022 and threatens to leave only remnants of glaciers by 2100, impacting vital European river systems.

title: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman shares the 11 principles every team member must sign
content: Microsoft has significantly bolstered its AI ambitions by hiring Mustafa Suleyman and Karen Simonyan, co-founders of Inflection AI, along with other key personnel. Suleyman, a DeepMind co-founder, now leads Microsoft's consumer AI division and a new MAI Superintelligence Team focused on practical, humanity-serving AI. This move follows Meta's substantial investments in AI talent, highlighting a fierce race for advanced artificial intelligence capabilities.

title: Rajkumar Hirani on rumours of a film with Ranbir Kapoor: 'No, I'm not making anything with him'
content: Filmmaker Rajkumar Hirani has dismissed rumors regarding a new film featuring Ranbir Kapoor, reassuring fans that no plans are currently in motion. He did share exciting news, revealing that sequels to '3 Idiots' and 'Munna Bhai' are in the works, with scripts being fine-tuned. Meanwhile, Hirani is set to venture into streaming with 'Pritam And Pedro' on JioHotstar, marking the screen debut of his son Vir.

title: JD Vance defends US strikes on Iran after cargo ship attack, says 'violence will be met with violence'
content: US Vice President JD Vance declared "violence will be met with violence" following American strikes on Iranian military sites. These retaliatory attacks targeted missile and drone facilities near the Strait of Hormuz, a direct response to Iran's drone strike on a cargo ship. Vance accused Tehran of violating a ceasefire agreement, while Iran's Ebrahim Azizi termed the action "ceasefire management," denying escalation.

title: Couple denied boarding despite valid passes, spent night at airport; airline to pay Rs 62,000
content: SpiceJet has been ordered by a Srinagar consumer commission to pay Rs 62,078 to a Haj couple denied boarding despite having valid passes. The airline, failing to appear, was found guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practice. The couple, returning from pilgrimage, faced a night at the airport and rebooked flights at a higher cost due to the airline's inexplicable refusal to let them board.

title: US approves limited rollout of Anthropic's Mythos 5 AI model
content: US authorities have partially lifted restrictions on Anthropic's advanced AI model, Mythos 5, granting access to select cybersecurity firms. This follows earlier concerns over national security vulnerabilities. The government, citing improved safeguards, has allowed a limited rollout, with ongoing discussions to broaden access and potentially restore public availability of its less powerful Fable 5 model. This development underscores the evolving regulatory landscape for cutting-edge AI.

title: Caitlin Clark injury update: Latest on Indiana Fever star as she hit with major setback
content: Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark has been ruled out of Saturday's game against the Los Angeles Sparks because of a recurring back injury, coach Stephanie White confirmed Friday. Clark aggravated the issue during Wednesday's loss to the Phoenix Mercury, creating fresh uncertainty about her availability. Although White said Clark is "OK," there is no firm timeline for her return.

title: No debut, but loud cheers: Belfast crowd shows love for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut is still pending, but the focus shifted to Ireland's historic 34-run victory in the first T20I.  Despite the loss, Shreyas Iyer vowed a strong comeback. Ireland's debutants impressed, with Jai Moondra and Matt Hollard making immediate impacts, while Abhishek Sharma's quickfire fifty wasn't enough for India.

title: ‘Varanasi’ update: Rajamouli wraps IMAX action scenes; shoot to complete by October
content: Filmmaker SS Rajamouli has revealed significant progress on his ambitious Rs 1,400 crore film, 'Varanasi'. He confirmed that all major IMAX action sequences are now complete, with smaller connecting scenes being filmed. The director stated shooting should wrap by September or October. The time-travel epic, starring Mahesh Babu and Priyanka Chopra, is set for an April 2027 release and was filmed in IMAX to capture its grand scale.

title: India's long‑range rockets can pick-off terror targets deep inside enemy territory
content: India is revolutionising its defence strategy with advanced long-range rocket systems like Pinaka and the upcoming Suryastra. These weapons are transforming from battlefield support to strategic assets, capable of precision strikes deep into enemy territory. This shift towards non-contact warfare emphasizes disrupting enemy operations from a distance, minimising troop exposure, and enhancing India's military modernization. The development signifies a move towards technology-driven dominance in future conflicts.

title: Air Force corporal held for Rs 1.5cr jewellery store heist
content: An Indian Air Force corporal, Deepak Singh Bhuker, has been arrested for a daring Rs 1.5 crore jewellery heist in Ahmedabad two years ago. Allegedly driven by gambling debts, Bhuker used a stolen service revolver and employed elaborate tactics, including fake IDs and avoiding fingerprints, to evade capture. Police also linked him to the theft of the weapon in Jamnagar, solving both cases.

title: 'Can't believe it': Ex-England captain stunned by Vaibhav Sooryavanshi snub
content: Michael Vaughan questioned India's decision to bench Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after their surprising T20I loss to Ireland. Despite a strong bowling performance initially, Ireland posted 182, with late hitting proving crucial. India's chase faltered, with only Abhishek Sharma showing brief resistance, ultimately falling short by 34 runs. The defeat has sparked debate over team selection, particularly the omission of the young batting prodigy.

title: Yuto Nagatomo first Asian player to play in five FIFA World Cups
content: In a groundbreaking achievement, Yuto Nagatomo has made history as the first Asian footballer to play in five FIFA World Cups. At 39, the Japanese defender's extraordinary career began in 2010, culminating in his appearance at the 2026 tournament. Nagatomo's relentless dedication and fitness now align him with legends like Ronaldo and Messi, marking a significant milestone for Asian representation in global football.

title: 'Welcome to the Jungle' BO collection: Film crosses Rs 29 cr worldwide on day 1
content: Ahmed Khan's 'Welcome to the Jungle' kicked off with a modest yet promising response, raking in Rs 18.75 crore net on its debut day. Viewers appreciated the dose of nostalgia and humor, though some critics pointed out its heavy reliance on references to earlier actor performances. Starring Akshay Kumar in a double role, this ensemble boasts an impressive cast. Read on to know more.

title: Travis Kelce lookalike wanted in purse theft case sparks viral frenzy
content: The Salem Police Department is searching for a man accused of stealing a purse and using the victim's credit cards after surveillance photos showed a suspect who closely resembles Chiefs star Travis Kelce. In a viral Facebook post, police joked about the resemblance and referenced Taylor Swift, writing, "Na, it can’t be. He’s a Swifty. He wouldn’t do this." Meanwhile, the real Kelce was in Nashville hosting Tight End University alongside a surprise appearance from his fiancée, Taylor Swift.

title: D4vd murder case: Prosecutors reveal alleged motive before July hearing
content: The criminal case involving singer D4vd remains active in California. Prosecutors allege he killed 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez to stop her from exposing an alleged relationship, while the defense strongly denies those claims. Burke has pleaded not guilty, and the court will continue hearing the case, with a preliminary hearing scheduled for July 21, 2026.

title: Rangers trade Brett Berard to the Montreal Canadiens for William Trudeau
content: The New York Rangers traded Brett Berard to the Montreal Canadiens for William Trudeau on June 26, ending a stalled fit for the 23-year-old forward. The deal gives Berard a cleaner path in Montreal and gives New York a left-shot defenseman after moving a player who faced waiver pressure and limited NHL runway.

title: FIFA World Cup: Record-breaker Messi rested for Argentina's group finale vs Jordan
content: To manage Lionel Messi's workload, Argentina's coach Lionel Scaloni has decided to bench the captain for the last Group J match against Jordan. This tactical choice comes after their team has already clinched the top position, focusing instead on keeping Messi fit for the intense knockout stages, especially following his notable injury and outstanding performances.

title: Bhabi Ji Ghar Par Hai actress Shubhangi Atre opens up on loneliness in industry
content: In an insightful revelation, Shubhangi Atre, famed for her role as Angoori Bhabi, shared her experiences of loneliness within the entertainment world. She believes this loneliness is not just about being alone, but rather the absence of substantial dialogue. Stating that true empathy outweighs mere advice, she remarked on social media's inability to forge real connections.

title: 920 dead, over 3,000 injured: Venezuela quake toll rises as rescuers race to find survivors
content: Venezuela grapples with a devastating toll of 920 dead and nearly 3,360 injured following twin earthquakes. La Guaira state is severely impacted, with thousands missing and millions potentially affected. International rescue teams and aid, including India's 'Operation Amistad' and US assistance, are now arriving to support desperate search-and-rescue efforts amidst widespread destruction and fear of aftershocks.

title: US, Israel & Lebanon sign trilateral framework deal after fifth round of talks
content: Israel, Lebanon, and the US have inked a trilateral accord to de-escalate border tensions, marking a significant step towards peace. Under the deal, Israel will withdraw from two southern Lebanese areas, with the Lebanese Armed Forces taking charge as part of a pilot security plan. Officials hailed it as a breakthrough, though challenges remain. Hezbollah has rejected the agreement, criticizing it as unilateral concessions by Lebanon.

title: "If She Didn't Like Ketan...": Pune Fort Murder Accused's Brother To Cops
content: Police allege that Ketan Agarwal was pushed off a cliff at Lohagad Fort on June 18 by his fiance Siya Goyal, 20 and her alleged lover Chetan Chaudhary, 22.

title: Terror Plot Averted In Assam, 2 Members Of Banned Outfit ULFA(I) Arrested
content: Security personnel recovered a significant cache of arms, ammunition, and survival equipment from their possession.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: "Violence Will Be Met With Violence," Says JD Vance As US Strikes Iran
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart, Masoud Pezeshkian, signed a 14-point deal to end the Middle East war on June 17.

title: Video: US Releases Footage Of Strikes On Iran, Then A Warning By Tehran
content: The US said the strikes on Iran were a "powerful response" to Tehran's attack on a commercial ship in Strait of Hormuz on June 25.

title: Indian National Among 8 Gets US Sanction For 'Fueling' Sudan Civil War
content: Among sanctioned individuals is Alok Choudhari of Raipur, CEO of SBL Energy Limited which supplied 200 shipments of explosives and explosive-related materials to a firm that maintained SAF's arsenal.

title: 'No Proof': HDFC On Ex-Chairman Who Resigned Over "Ethical Differences"
content: The three-month legal review found no evidence from board committee minutes or witness interviews that Chakraborty raised concerns that "happenings and practices" at the bank were not aligned with his personal "values and ethics".

title: US Forces Strike Iranian Drone, Missile Sites After Attack On Cargo Ship
content: US forces struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar locations on Friday in response to an Iranian attack on a cargo ship, the US military said.

title: 4 Days On,  50,000 Still Missing After Venezuela Earthquakes, Deaths At 920
content: The coastal area of La Guaira, near the capital Caracas, was the worst hit, with one building after another crumpled by the magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 quakes.

title: Trump Thanks India After Hyderabad Renames Road After Him
content: Trump's remarks on Truth Social on Friday came after the Telangana government named a road adjoining the US Consulate in the southern Indian city as "Donald Trump Avenue".

title: Siachen Base Camp To Get Power Grid For First Time By September: Lt Governor
content: Zanskar and Nubra are also set to receive grid connectivity for the first time under transmission projects targeted for completion by September 15.

title: Iran Says It Targeted US Positions In The Region In Response To Attack
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guards on Saturday said it targeted US military positions in the region, in response to a fresh U.S. strike against Iran.

title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: New 4.9 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Venezuela
content: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.

title: Days After Twin Quakes, Another Earthquake Hits Venezuela
content: A new earthquake struck off the northern coast of Venezuela on Friday afternoon, days after twin quakes flattened buildings and killed nearly 1,000 people.

title: "They Shouldn't Be Doing That": Trump After Iran's Attack On Ship In Hormuz
content: US President Donald Trump on Friday said that one will find out if there will be consequences for Iran's violation of the ceasefire agreement.

title: Hezbollah Rejects Israel-Lebanon Deal, Warns Of "Civil War"
content: Israel and Lebanon hailed an agreement signed Friday with the United States to pave a way towards peace on their front in the Middle East war, while Iran-backed Hezbollah warned the deal would thwart plans to resolve the broader conflict.

title: "Foolish Violation": Trump Says Iran Fired 4 Drones At Ships In Hormuz, 1 Hit
content: In a post on Truth Social, Trump said Iran had shot at least four one-way drones on ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Trump Blasts "Godless" Democrats In Incendiary Speech To Evangelicals
content: Speaking in Washington after the success of three progressive candidates in New York's Democratic primary elections this week, Trump called left-wing ideology an "uncontrollable form of cancer" that will ensure America is "taken down."

title: JD Vance Says Watergate Would Be '12-Hour News Story' Today
content: US Vice President JD Vance says it is "crazy" that the Watergate scandal brought down Richard Nixon's presidency, arguing that the political spying affair would dominate headlines for no more than a few hours.

title: Punjab BJP Chief Praises Maharashtra's Move To Consult Sikhs On Takht Law
content: His remarks came as Punjab leaders, including former Union minister Harsimrat Kaur Badal, Congress MP Sukhjinder Singh Randhawa and Akali Dal Waris Punjab De leader Manpreet Singh Ayali, opposed the proposed law.

title: Katchatheevu island transfer to Sri Lanka ‘dark chapter’ of Congress: BJP
content: BJP fired a twin salvo at the party and alleged its decisions have harmed the interests of the two states . it is the 52nd anniversary of the decision of the then Congress govt at the centre to cede Katchatheevu island to Sri Lanka . BJP said it is another blackspot in Congress’ calendar of surrenders .

title: Make use of FTAs, other schemes to boost shipments, government tells businesses
content: govt has asked businesses to make use of free trade agreements (FTAs) and schemes beyond interest subsidy, which are part of the export promotion mission to boost shipments from the country . the emphasis comes amid a flurry of trade deals, with the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement due to be implemented from July 15 .

title: Humble Re 1 retains coining glory despite rise of digital payments
content: Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: Loss-making Air India stops crew laundry services on international layovers
content: the facility will continue during domestic layovers . pilots and cabin crew get their uniforms laundered at layover hotels . air India group incurring losses of thousands of crores last fiscal . e-mail sent to pilots: "this represents a change from a long-standing arrangement"

title: Homebuyers can seek relief for delay after possession: Supreme Court
content: the SC set aside an order of national consumer Disputes Redressal Commission . it held that ahomebuyer ceases to be a consumer after taking possession of the flat . the arbitration clause in homebuyer-real estate company agreement would not block the former from approaching the consumer forum .

title: 'First US Prez to be honoured': Trump thanks India after Hyderabad renames road after him

title: Beijing expands Indian Ocean footprint with Bangladesh port deal
content: the land for the zone at mongla was initially earmarked for India . it was delisted by the Muhammad Yunus interim govt in 2025 . the project could expand China’s strategic footprint in Bay of Bengal and wider Indian Ocean region .

title: 'By mistake crossed the US-Canada border': Indian man claims friend detained by authorities
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Government reveals names of six bravehearts martyred in Operation Sindoor
content: the names of the six bravehearts were released alongside the list of all soldiers who made the supreme sacrifice during various military operations in 2025 . two of the martyrs have received gallantry awards . the govt has released the names for the first time since its launch in may last year .

title: HDFC review finds no evidence backing ex-chairman’s charges
content: Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: Lohagad murder: Accused Siya Goyal says she disliked fiancé Ketan Agarwal over 'wig'
content: siya Goyal reportedly told police she disliked her fiancé, Ketan Agarwal, because he wore a hair patch, police said . she said she didn't want to hurt her family's sentiments . her father, Vishal, dismissed the claim as an absurd justification .
